name: Eretza and Grazal Stream Loop
name_original: Eretza desde Sodupe — circular por la senda del Arroyo Grazal
description: Hike the moderate Eretza and Grazal Stream Loop, a 7.2-mile route with 2583 feet of gain, featuring stream views and summit vistas.
region_intro: Hiking around Güeñes offers diverse terrain within the comarca of Las Encartaciones in the Basque Country. The municipality is characterized by a narrow valley carved by the Cadagua River, framed by mountains such as Ubieta (1,115 m), Ganekogorta (998 m), and Eretza (880 m). This landscape provides a range of hiking opportunities, from river valley paths to challenging mountain ascents. Best hiking trails around Güeñes The most popular hiking route is Eretza and Grazal Stream Loop, a 7.2 miles (11.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 23 minutes to complete. This moderate route ascends towards Eretza mountain, offering views of the surrounding landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Hike from Zaramillo — circular via the Ruta de las Minas and Seles (PR-BI 100.2), a difficult 11.0 miles (17.7 km) path. This circular route explores historical mining areas and varied terrain. Local hikers also love the Grazal Stream Trail – Hermitage of Saint Bartholomew loop from Sodupe, a 5.4 miles (8.8 km) trail leading through the Cadagua River valley, often completed in about 2 hours 37 minutes. Hiking around Güeñes is defined by the Cadagua River valley, surrounding mountains like Gallarraga and Eretza, and historical elements including parts of the Camino de Santiago. The network offers options for different ability levels, from gentle river walks to demanding mountain climbs. The routes in Güeñes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 220 reviews. More than 1,300 hikers have used komoot to explore Güeñes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Güeñes?
A: Güeñes offers a wide variety of hiking trails, with over 30 routes documented on komoot. These range from easy strolls along the Cadagua River to challenging mountain ascents, catering to all fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly walks in Güeñes?
A: Yes, Güeñes has several easy routes suitable for families. The area features gentle paths along the Cadagua River valley. For a pleasant moderate option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3991567" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grazal Stream Trail – Hermitage of Saint Bartholomew loop from Sodupe&lt;/a&gt;, which is 5.4 miles (8.8 km) and takes about 2 hours 37 minutes.

Q: What are the options for challenging day hikes in Güeñes?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Güeñes offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1307075111/hike-from-zaramillo-circular-via-the-ruta-de-las-minas-and-seles-pr-bi-100-2" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hike from Zaramillo — circular via the Ruta de las Minas and Seles (PR-BI 100.2)&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 11.0 miles (17.7 km) path exploring historical mining areas. Another strenuous option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5343777" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pagero Summit – Ganekogorta Summit loop from La Quadra&lt;/a&gt;, which involves significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there any circular walks in Güeñes?
A: Many of the trails in Güeñes are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037641664/eretza-and-grazal-stream-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eretza and Grazal Stream Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which ascends towards Eretza mountain, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3991553" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gallarraga Summit (901 m) – Aguilatos Summit (1,677 m) loop from Sodupe&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see while hiking in Güeñes?
A: Güeñes is characterized by the narrow valley of the Cadagua River and is framed by notable mountains like Ubieta, Ganekogorta, and Eretza. You'll encounter diverse landscapes, from serene riverbanks to challenging mountain terrain with scenic viewpoints. The area also features the English-style Arenaza Park, known for its diverse plant species and avant-garde sculptures.

Q: What historical or cultural attractions can I find along the trails in Güeñes?
A: Beyond natural beauty, Güeñes boasts a rich historical heritage. You can explore sites like the 16th-century Church of Santa María de Güeñes, the Renaissance-style Amezaga Palace, and medieval tower-houses such as De la Quadra Tower and Sologuchi Tower. The municipality is also part of the Camino de Santiago, offering paths with historical significance.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or summits worth hiking to in Güeñes?
A: Absolutely. The mountains surrounding Güeñes offer fantastic viewpoints. You can hike to summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/558197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ganekogorta Summit&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/629426" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eretza Summit (887 m)&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic views of the region. These peaks provide a rewarding experience for those seeking elevated perspectives.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Güeñes?
A: The trails in Güeñes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 220 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, from the peaceful river valley walks to the challenging mountain ascents, and the well-maintained paths that allow for diverse outdoor experiences.

Q: Are there any lakes or reservoirs to visit near Güeñes?
A: Yes, the area around Güeñes features several interesting water bodies. You can explore highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4096819" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo Hostión&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2856350" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorostiza Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2939178" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lakes of the Old Iron Mines&lt;/a&gt;, which offer unique natural settings for a visit.

Q: Is it possible to access hiking trails in Güeñes using public transport?
A: While specific public transport connections to trailheads vary, Güeñes is part of the larger Basque Country region, which generally has public transport options. For detailed information on reaching specific starting points, it's advisable to check local bus or train schedules connecting to towns like Sodupe or Zaramillo, which are common starting points for routes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Güeñes?
A: Güeñes offers hiking opportunities throughout the year. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes, ideal for exploring. Summer can be warm, especially on exposed mountain trails, while winter offers a different, often quieter, experience, though some higher routes may require appropriate gear for colder conditions.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Eretza and Grazal Stream Loop?
faqs.answer: The Eretza and Grazal Stream Loop typically starts from Sodupe in Bizkaia. You can usually find parking within Sodupe itself, making it a convenient starting point for the hike.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While the trail is considered moderate with significant elevation gain (around 790 meters), some sections feature easily accessible paths. There are variations, including shorter routes like the 'Grazal Stream Trail – Hermitage of Saint Bartholomew loop' which is less demanding. Hikers should have a good fitness level for the full loop, but less experienced hikers might enjoy shorter segments.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers varied terrain, including unpaved natural paths, asphalt, and paved sections. Some parts may require sure-footedness, especially with the elevation changes, but generally, the paths are accessible.

faqs.question: What notable natural features and landmarks will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience diverse scenery, with a significant highlight being the Grazal Stream Trail itself. The route also leads to the Eretza Summit (887 m), offering panoramic views. At the foot of Mount Eretza, you'll find the La Berenilla Refuge.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several official trails, including Grazal, Luxar - Eretza, Camino Olvidado (Bilbao-Villasante), Burdin Hesia, Via Verde del Kadagua, and various segments of Subida a Eretza and Senda del arroyo Grazal.

faqs.question: Is the Eretza and Grazal Stream Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are welcome on trails in this region. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock, and to practice Leave No Trace principles by cleaning up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this loop?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed during most seasons. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, so an early start is advisable. Winter hiking is possible, but be prepared for potentially colder conditions and check local weather forecasts for snow or ice, especially at higher elevations like the Eretza Summit.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail or the Montes de Triano park?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Eretza and Grazal Stream Loop or to access the Montes de Triano park where it is located. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or pubs near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: As the trail starts near Sodupe, you can find various amenities such as cafes, pubs, and shops within the town before or after your hike. It's a good idea to stock up on water and snacks there.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, it's recommended to bring sturdy hiking boots, sufficient water, snacks, sun protection, and appropriate layers of clothing. A map or GPS device is also advisable, especially for navigating different trail sections.

faqs.question: Are there any viewpoints or specific spots for scenic photography?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! The Eretza Summit (887 m) provides excellent panoramic views of the surrounding landscape. Additionally, sections along the Grazal Stream Trail offer picturesque natural settings perfect for photography.
